---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[
Exploring the Success Stories (II)]

- "Green" taxes addressing environmental problems
]

<br>

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases
    - https://docs.google.com/document/d/17Bkakj7I3D1CcDnE85TfiaLTwvU3Z7ym4gKe0855C48/edit?usp=sharing


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 2
.size45[
Which of the four policy design options is the "right" choice for addressing your environmental problem?

- Analyze the pros and cons of each option for your chosen problem, and

- Support all claims with evidence
]

???

Keep your eye on the next target: Paper 2.

- In that paper you will be asked to evaluate all four of the approaches we study in class from the perspective of your chosen environmental problem.

### Any questions on the prompt?



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[**Today: "Green" Tax Cases**]

.size45[
Find a real world example of .textblue[**this approach**] being used to .textblue[**successfully**] address an environmental problem .textblue[**similar to the one in your project**].
]

.size45[
.textblue[**Present as an argument**]: This case shows that addressing environmental problem X can be done successfully using "green" taxes.
]

???

### Does everybody have an argument to present and discuss today?

<br>

Let's review all the cases for APA format.



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size45[
This case shows that using a "green" tax we can address environmental problem X.

- What specific, narrow aspect of your problem does this tax focus on?

- Which stakeholders does it target? What does it require them to do?

- What institutions support this tax?
]

???

Take a moment to reflect on your argument and get ready to present it to other people along these three dimensions?

### Questions on this?

Get to it!

<br>

Let's start with some speed dating to warm up!

Pair off (10 mins): Each person take 5 minutes to present the case argument you brought for today.

<br>

New pairs, do it again! (10 mins)

<br>

Ok, at this point you've heard two different arguments and hopefully gotten some time to reflect on your own case argument.

Now let's try to extract lessons from all of them.

Everybody form groups of (three? four?) and spread out around the room.
- Try to form groups with people you didn't pair off with during the warm-up!



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**"green" taxation**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

???

Here's the over-arching goal of our group-led evaluation.

Groups compare and contrast each set of answers and help the class extract generalizable lessons to help us evaluate green taxes.

### Make sense?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**"green" taxation**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
1) How specifically do these taxes target the problem? 
]

???

Ok, groups, first prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the taxes target the problem.

Any environmental problem is likely to be large and multi-faceted, but I'm willing to bet these taxes are highly targeted.

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Problem Targeting'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**"green" taxation**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
2) What kinds of stakeholders do these taxes target? 

- Any carrots included or purely a stick?
]

???

Ok, groups, second prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the taxes target the stakeholders.

- Are the taxes purely punitive or are there also incentives included?

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Stakeholder Targeting'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**"green" taxation**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
3) What kinds of local institutions exist to support and/or reinforce these taxes? 
]

???

Ok, groups, final prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the taxes fit into the established local system.

- Are the taxes supported by institutional actors or built into established processes?

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Institutional Support'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/11-1-Green_taxation_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: top

.size55[**Best advice / things to remember when designing "green" taxes?**]

???

Bottom line time.

### In a practical sense, what guidance can we offer policy-makers considering using a "green" tax approach to solving environmental problems?

- ?

<br>

**Ok, everybody take five minutes to reflect on our work today.**

Jot down some notes that I promise you'll need when it comes time to write the next paper.

What are the pros and cons of using this approach for your specific problem?


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/12-1-Green_Subsidies.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: center

???

Next week we explore our next policy design, green subsidies!

Make sure you consolidate and clean up your notes from this week.

You might end up making green taxes central to your paper!
